questions.question: What does 'entropy' measure in information theory?
questions.questionZh: 信息论（Information Theory）中的“熵”（Entropy）衡量的是什么？
options: The energy of a physical system
The average surprise or uncertainty in a probability distribution
The number of bits in a binary message
The error rate of a communication channel
optionsZh: 物理系统 (Physical System) 的能量 (Energy)
概率分布（Probability Distribution）中的平均意外程度（Surprise）或不确定性（Uncertainty）
二进制消息 (binary message) 中的比特 (bits) 数量
通信信道 (Communication Channel) 的错误率 (Error Rate)
questions.explanation: Entropy H(P) = -sum(p(x) * log(p(x))) measures the average amount of surprise across all outcomes. High entropy means high uncertainty (uniform distribution). Low entropy means the outcome is predictable.
questions.explanationZh: 熵（Entropy）H(P) = -sum(p(x) * log(p(x))) 衡量了所有可能结果的平均意外程度。高熵意味着高不确定性（均匀分布），低熵则意味着结果是可预测的。

questions.question: What is the cross-entropy loss commonly used for in neural networks?
questions.questionZh: 交叉熵损失（Cross-Entropy Loss）在神经网络（Neural Networks）中通常用于什么？
options: Regression tasks with continuous outputs
Classification tasks, measuring how far predicted probabilities are from true labels
Generating new data samples
Regularizing model weights to prevent overfitting
optionsZh: 具有连续输出（continuous outputs）的回归任务（Regression tasks）
分类任务（Classification tasks），用于衡量预测概率（Predicted Probabilities）与真实标签（True Labels）之间的差异。
生成新数据样本
正则化（Regularization）模型权重以防止过拟合（Overfitting）
questions.explanation: Cross-entropy loss H(P,Q) = -sum(p(x)*log(q(x))) measures the difference between the true distribution (labels) and the model's predicted distribution. It is THE standard loss for classification.
questions.explanationZh: 交叉熵损失 (Cross-entropy loss) H(P,Q) = -sum(p(x)*log(q(x))) 用于衡量真实分布（标签）与模型预测分布之间的差异。它是分类任务中标准的损失函数。

questions.question: Why is minimizing cross-entropy equivalent to minimizing KL divergence during training?
questions.questionZh: 为什么在训练过程中，最小化交叉熵（Cross-Entropy）等价于最小化KL散度（KL Divergence）？
options: Because KL divergence is always zero during training
Because cross-entropy = entropy + KL divergence, and the entropy of the true labels is constant, so minimizing cross-entropy minimizes KL divergence
Because cross-entropy and KL divergence are the same formula
Because the model's entropy equals zero at convergence
optionsZh: 由于在训练过程中 KL散度 (KL divergence) 始终为零。
由于交叉熵（Cross-Entropy）等于熵（Entropy）与KL散度（KL Divergence）之和，且真实标签（True Labels）的熵为常数，因此最小化交叉熵也就最小化了KL散度。
因为交叉熵（Cross-Entropy）与KL散度（KL Divergence）实际上是同一个公式。
由于模型在收敛 (convergence) 时，其熵 (entropy) 等于零。
questions.explanation: H(P,Q) = H(P) + D_KL(P||Q). Since the true distribution P doesn't change during training, H(P) is constant. Minimizing H(P,Q) is the same as minimizing D_KL(P||Q) -- pushing the model toward the true distribution.
questions.explanationZh: `H(P,Q) = H(P) + D_KL(P||Q)`。由于在训练过程中真实分布（True Distribution）P 保持不变，因此 `H(P)` 为常数。最小化 `H(P,Q)` 等同于最小化 KL散度（Kullback-Leibler Divergence）`D_KL(P||Q)`，即促使模型不断逼近真实分布。

questions.question: A language model has perplexity 50 on a test set. What does this mean?
questions.questionZh: 某语言模型（Language Model）在测试集（Test Set）上的困惑度（Perplexity）为 50。这意味着什么？
options: The model makes 50 errors per sentence
On average, the model is as uncertain as if it were choosing uniformly from 50 possible next tokens at each step
The model has 50 layers
The model was trained for 50 epochs
optionsZh: 该模型 (model) 每句会产生 50 个错误。
平均而言，该模型的不确定性相当于在每一步都从50个可能的下一个词元（token）中均匀随机选择。
该模型（Model）共有 50 层（Layers）。
该模型共训练了 50 个轮次 (epoch)。
questions.explanation: Perplexity = e^(cross-entropy). A perplexity of 50 means the model's uncertainty at each token is equivalent to picking randomly from 50 equally likely options. Lower perplexity means better predictions.
questions.explanationZh: 困惑度 (Perplexity) = e^(交叉熵 (cross-entropy))。困惑度为 50 表示模型在每个词元 (token) 上的不确定性，相当于从 50 个等概率的选项中随机选择一个。困惑度越低，意味着预测效果越好。

questions.question: How does mutual information differ from Pearson correlation for feature selection?
questions.questionZh: 在特征选择（Feature Selection）中，互信息（Mutual Information）与皮尔逊相关系数（Pearson Correlation）有何区别？
options: Mutual information is faster to compute
Mutual information detects any statistical dependency (linear or nonlinear) while correlation only detects linear relationships
Pearson correlation works for any data type while mutual information only works for continuous data
They always give the same feature rankings
optionsZh: 互信息（Mutual Information）的计算速度更快。
互信息（Mutual Information）能够检测任意统计依赖关系（线性或非线性），而相关性（Correlation）仅能检测线性关系。
皮尔逊相关系数 (Pearson correlation) 适用于任意数据类型，而互信息 (mutual information) 仅适用于连续数据。
它们始终会给出相同的特征排序 (feature ranking)。
questions.explanation: Mutual information I(X;Y) captures all statistical dependencies between variables, including nonlinear and non-monotonic ones. Pearson correlation only measures linear association, missing many important relationships.
questions.explanationZh: 互信息 (Mutual Information) I(X;Y) 能够捕捉变量之间的所有统计依赖关系，包括非线性和非单调的关系。皮尔逊相关系数 (Pearson correlation) 仅衡量线性关联，会遗漏许多重要的关系。
